title: Crossing the Gap
editorialSummary: Bridge types grouped by structural principle. False trail: Rope Bridge and Cable-Stayed Bridge both use cables but differ in principle — a rope bridge is a simple flexible span with no rigid deck, while a cable-stayed bridge uses diagonal cables from towers to support a rigid deck. Pontoon Bridge floats on water and is not cable-supported despite sometimes resembling one visually. Bascule bridges are movable (they open like a drawbridge); Swing Bridge and Lift Bridge are also movable — all three are grouped as Movable Bridges. Truss bridges are distinct from girder bridges in using a framework of triangular units.

meta.gptRationale: Bridge terminology is technical and several items sound structurally similar, making the group boundaries easy to blur.
